response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 16+ years of experience, they are fundamentally misaligned with the core technical requirements of this role. The position explicitly requires deep C++ and STL expertise for architecting high-performance industrial automation systems; this candidate's primary expertise over the past decade is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack development. Their last significant C++ work was in 2014–2015, and there is no evidence of industrial automation, IoT, secure development lifecycle practices, IEC 62443 standards, or MQTT protocol experience. Additionally, the resume contains identity inconsistencies (name 'Mohammed Al-Said' vs. email/GitHub 'szabobogdan'), which warrant verification before further consideration.
strengths: 16+ years of software engineering experience significantly exceeding the 8–12 year requirement, with demonstrated expertise across multiple platforms
Proven leadership and mentoring ability through workshops, courses, and Refactoring Dojos; staff-level engineering demonstrates seniority alignment
Full-stack technical depth and experience architecting complex distributed systems, including event-driven microservices architecture
concerns: C++ expertise is minimal and outdated; last C++ work was 2014–2015 at National Instruments; primary recent focus (past 10 years) is TypeScript/Node.js web development
Complete absence of industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or security-critical systems background; no mention of MQTT, IEC 62443, SDL practices, or secure coding standards
Resume identity inconsistency: name listed as 'Mohammed Al-Said' but email, GitHub, and LinkedIn all reference 'Szabo Bogdan' or 'gedaiu,' raising authentication and legitimacy concerns

key_factors.factor: C++ Systems Programming and STL Expertise
Industrial Automation and Cybersecurity Standards Knowledge
